introduction: A clinical syndrome resulting from bacterial blood stream infection with active proliferation of the organisms. Early-Onset sepsis manifests within the first 72 hours of life or at most 7 days of life while Late-Onset sepsis manifests after 7 days of life.
investigations: A lumbar tap for cerebrospinal fluid analysis is mandatory unless the infant has cardio-respiratory instability. It must not delay empirical antibiotic therapy.
Neuro-imaging studies if fever persists, seizures recur, or occipitofrontal circumference is increasing.

drug: The empirical antibiotic therapy tailored to local patterns.
Ceftriaxone 50 mg/kg 12-hourly or Cefotaxime 50 mg/kg 6-hourly and Gentamicin 2.5 mg/kg 12-hourly.
Intravenous Ampicillin 50 mg/kg 6-hourly in specific cases.
Phenobarbitone for seizures; 15 mg/kg loading dose, 2.5 mg/kg 12-hourly maintenance.
supportive_measures: Fluid therapy with caution against over-hydration.
Fluid and caloric balance, blood transfusion for severe anaemia.
Oxygen therapy and ventilator support for hypoxaemia.
